title: Woone Smile Mwore
content:
O! MARY, when the zun went down, 
Woone night in spring, wï¿½ viry rim, 
Behind the nap wiï¿½ woody crown, 
Anï¿½ left your smilen face so dim; 
Your little sister there, inside,
Wiï¿½ bellows on her little knee, 
Did blow the vire, a-glearen wide 
Drough window-panes, that I could zee,Â Â Â Â 
As you did stanï¿½ wiï¿½ me, avore 
The house, a-parten, woone smile mwore.

The chattï¿½ren birds, a-risen high, 
Anï¿½ zinken low, did swiftly vlee 
Vrom shrinken moss, a-growen dry, 
Upon the lanen apple tree. 
Anï¿½ there the dog, a-whippen wide
His hairy tail, anï¿½ comen near, 
Did fondly lay agan you zide 
His coal-black nose anï¿½ russet ear: 
To win what I ï¿½d a-won avore, 
Vrom your gay; face, his woone smile mwore.

Anï¿½ while your mother bustled sprack, 
A-getten supper out in hall, 
Anï¿½ cast her shade, a-whivï¿½ren black 
Avore the vire, upon the wall; 
Your brother come, wiï¿½ easy pace, 
In drough the slammen gate, along 
The path, wiï¿½ healthy-bloomen face, 
A-whisï¿½len shrill his last new zong: 
Anï¿½ when he come avore the door, 
He met vrom you his woone smile mwore. 

Now you that wer the daughter there, 
Be mother on a husbandï¿½s vloor, 
Anï¿½ mid ye meet wiï¿½ less oï¿½ care 
Than what your harty mother bore; 
Anï¿½ if abroad I have to rue 
The bitter tongue, or wrongvul deed, 
Mid I come hwome to share wiï¿½ you 
What ï¿½s needvul free oï¿½ pinchen need: 
Anï¿½ vind that you haï¿½ still in store 
My evenen meal, anï¿½ woone smile mwore.
meaning:
This evocative piece by William Barnes represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of William Barnes's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.
